mod.name: Closed Loop Ecology
mod.description:
Realistic carbon, water, and nitrogen cycles for Space Haven.
Vanilla's life-support economy has three structural breaks: - Composting biomass loses 87% of its mass to the accounting void - Carbon is mineable but gated at Mining skill 7 (peer with Noble Metals) - Chemicals → Fertilizer can only be made from Rot, which requires corpses
Peaceful playthroughs can't close the food loop without trading.
This mod ships six surgical patches:
1. Carbon mining skill 7 → 2 (matches Base Metals); Raw Chemicals 6 → 3. Carbonaceous chondrite asteroids become early-game accessible.
2. Composter carbon retention ×6 (1 biomass → 0.10 carbon, matches real biochar yields of 30-70%).
3. CO2 scrubber rebalanced to the exact mathematical inverse of the carbon burner. The C ↔ CO2 round-trip is now mass-conservative — no more free-carbon exploit from looping burner + scrubber.
4. New recipe — Industrial Chemicals Synthesis (interactive at the existing Chemical Refinery): 1 Carbon + 3 Water → 3 Chemicals + 2 Oxygen Tanks. The tanks auto-vent to atmospheric O2 at any Life Support station. Real-world analog: Fischer-Tropsch synthesis plus water electrolysis. Closes the fertilizer cycle without combat dependency.
5. New recipe — Biomass Pyrolysis (interactive at Chemical Refinery): 2 Biomass → 1 Carbon + 1 Char Residue. Residue auto-vents to CO2 at the CO2 Scrubber. Real-world analog: industrial pyrolysis / charcoal production. Bypasses the lossy composter for high- throughput carbon refining.
6. Two-stage gas-byproduct pattern: gas outputs are bridged through transient inventory items (Oxygen Tank, Char Residue) so the interactive "produce if storage < X" condition keeps working on the crew-operated recipes, while the actual atmospheric release happens via non-interactive vents at appropriate life-support stations.
After install, a peaceful 5-crew ship can run an indefinite food cycle on the carbon they mine + the biomass they compost. Water remains the primary bottleneck (ice mining), as in vanilla.
mod.author: mingc
